---
title: "fastder-evaluation runtime and memory"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide
params:
  bench_dir: NULL
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 6,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.align = "center"
)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r load}
bench_dir <- params$bench_dir
if (is.null(bench_dir) || !dir.exists(bench_dir)) {
  stop("bench_dir parameter must point to an existing benchmarks directory")
}
files <- list.files(bench_dir, pattern = "\\.tsv$", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
if (length(files) == 0) {
  stop("no .tsv benchmark files found under ", bench_dir)
}
parse_one <- function(path) {
  rel <- sub(paste0(bench_dir, .Platform$file.sep), "", path)
  parts <- strsplit(rel, .Platform$file.sep, fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
  if (length(parts) == 1) {
    rule <- sub("\\.tsv$", "", parts[1])
    wc   <- ""
  } else {
    rule <- parts[1]
    wc   <- sub("\\.tsv$", "", paste(parts[-1], collapse = "_"))
  }
  d <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  d$rule <- rule
  d$wildcards <- wc
  d
}
df <- bind_rows(lapply(files, parse_one))
df$wall_s    <- as.numeric(df$s)
df$max_rss_mb <- as.numeric(df$max_rss)
```

## Inputs

`r bench_dir` (`r length(files)` files)

```{r table}
knitr::kable(df %>% select(rule, wildcards, wall_s, max_rss_mb) %>% arrange(rule, wildcards),
             digits = 1)
```

## Wall time per rule

Each point is one rule invocation. Rules run multiple times (for instance per
sample) appear as multiple points.

```{r time, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = rule, y = wall_s)) +
  geom_point() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw()
```

## Max resident memory per rule

Same data, switched axis.

```{r mem, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = rule, y = max_rss_mb)) +
  geom_point() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw()
```

## Wall time vs max memory

Each rule invocation as a single point. Useful for spotting rules that are
both slow and memory hungry.

```{r scatter, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 6}
ggplot(df, aes(x = wall_s, y = max_rss_mb, colour = rule)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_bw()
```
